channel.title: From Whale Island to the Pequod
channel.description: Recent content on From Whale Island to the Pequod

item.title: Somutun Kaybı ve Kırsal Belleğin İnşası
item.description: Bu yazı için çok da bilgi sahibi olmadığım bir konuda derin okumalar yaptım ve doğrudan hayat deneyimleri edindim. Daha da önemlisi, bu konu hakkında Türkiye’de belki de en fazla şey bilen birkaç genç entelektüelden biri olduğunu düşündüğüm Lara’ya (@kirsalbellek) beraber blogum için bir yazı yazma cürreti gösterdim. Ben sözü uzatmadan, köşeyi doğrudan Kırsal Bellek’in mutfağındaki Lara’ya bırakıyorum:

item.title: This Thing of Ours, The Borderline Era: The Sopranos
item.description: Tragedy is classically defined as a dramatic narrative in which a protagonist—often a figure of power—is brought to ruin or profound sorrow. This descent typically unfolds in three fundamental stages: first, the Hamartia (the fatal character flaw or poisoned roots that sow the seeds of destruction); second, the Peripeteia (the turning point or reversal, where the illusion of free will is tested and terrible choices are made); and finally, the Catastrophe (the ultimate ruin, the absolute loss of humanity, and the surrender to the void).

item.title: Living as Spartanian: Mani
item.description: Hello. Before leaving you alone with my Mani chronicles, which are full of literary references and a bit of theatricality, there is an important point I must mention: this piece is a semi-travelogue, semi-indistinguishable tale, translated directly back into English from the diary I tried to keep in Italian during my time in Greece. However, since my Italian is not one of my shining qualities, I often struggled to understand what I meant to say and tried to capture the meaning without damaging the writing. As in every good story, there is a bit of that within these as well. Now, the dear reader might ask: “O author, why are you turning these events you’ve just lived through from a diary into a travel summary?” Since I couldn’t think of a cleverly arrogant answer fast enough for them, those readers will certainly be answered in my future posts.

item.title: Wittgenstein's Tractatus and the Linear Space of Thought
item.description:
Note: This essay is a blog adaptation of a paper originally archived on Zenodo. Original DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.15798351

Abstract

This essay explores a conceptual analogy between Ludwig Wittgenstein’s Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus and the structure of linear vector spaces in mathematics. It proposes that the limits of language, as discussed in the Tractatus, can be modeled as projections from a high-dimensional conceptual space into lower-dimensional communicative subspaces. Drawing on elements from formal logic, linear algebra, and epistemology, the paper interprets key Tractarian concepts such as totality (Gesamtheit) and the say/show distinction through the lens of mathematical transformation. The analogy offers a novel framework for understanding the irreducibility of thought into language, the indeterminacy of reference, and the structural instability of certain philosophical questions. Rather than offering a metaphysical solution, the essay argues that some longstanding philosophical problems are symptomatic of linguistic overload or semantic compression, rather than deep ontological mystery. This perspective aims to bridge philosophical analysis with formal reasoning, suggesting a hybrid methodology for investigating the architecture of meaning.

item.title: Free Will and it's Impact to Our Life
item.description:
Hello lads. Welcome aboard the ship that sails between trivial pop culture, natural sciences, and eternal philosophical debates.

Before I start writing, I thought I should explain the basics: What will I write? Why am I doing this? And what I am expecting from it?

What will I write?

Mostly, about everything that I find interesting. In my daily life, I constantly see strange correlations between science, linguistics, pop culture, and literature. So I thought to myself: I should turn these abstract thought processes into something concrete.
